title: Kaworus connection to Ramiel
wikitext:
[]: Re: I might need Shin-seiki's help             Quote Post by V » Mon Jan 21, 2008 11:40 pm UrsusArctos wrote: Even if Ramiel didn't actually guard over Kaworu when he was floating in the Antarctic Dead Sea or elsewhere, there is a definite symbolic link between them, and Ramiel, with an immensely powerful AT field and a shoot-at-sight beam attack, would make for one hell of a guardian angel.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Jan 22, 2008 5:55 am Double post, for a reason. This one's on Ramiel. Ramiel's territory is pretty convoluted. From what I understand, Ramiel is also spelled Ramael and Remiel, and comes off alternately as a holy angel and as a fallen one as well. The name, oddly enough, means "Compassion of God" or "Beloved of God", being the Angel of Hope, although he's also called also called "Jeremiel" or "Uriel" and watches over Terror and Thunder as well. (Our Rami-san was given a straight association with thunder). Uriel, after all, was the protector Angel of John the Baptist, whom I've associated Kaworu with. There isn't any space to make an association between Ramiel and Kaworu in the TV series other than these background details (Apart from the fact that, during their respective attacks, they are quoted as having the most powerful A.T. Field ever), but there might be one in Rebuild. Ramiel serves both as an Angel of Divine Visions (Shinji had another one of those visions after Ramiel boiled him in Rebuild) and also as the Angel who guides souls to heaven, as "one of those holy angels whom God has set over those to rise from the dead". Rebuild spoilers here on- Kaworu rises out of a coffin on the moon, the third coffin to be opened. Since the other two open coffins might correspond to the other two Angels, Kaworu might correspond to Ramiel directly this time around. Since the Angel supposed to preside over resurrection has been resurrected in a human body himself, I doubt any more of those coffins are going to be open. Any guesses for who Sachi and Shammy's human vessels are going to be?

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Tue Jan 22, 2008 7:11 am UrsusArctos wrote: From what I understand, Ramiel is also spelled Ramael and Remiel,

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Jan 22, 2008 9:09 am I ran into the same trouble with the name on a Google search. For instance- http://www.greatdreams.com/archangels.htm Just enter "Ramiel Remiel" or "Remiel Ramiel" and you'll see what I mean. The wiki article, which I actually edited out a little bit, actually called Ramiel "Remiel" and then "Ramiel" again and then said "Don't confuse the two". What can I say, man? I hope you can help me with this...

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Tue Jan 22, 2008 9:47 am UrsusArctos wrote: Just enter "Ramiel Remiel" or "Remiel Ramiel" and you'll see what I mean. The wiki article, which I actually edited out a little bit, actually called Ramiel "Remiel" and then "Ramiel" again and then said "Don't confuse the two". What can I say, man? I hope you can help me with this...

[]:             Quote Post by V » Tue Jan 22, 2008 11:04 am (Apart from the fact that, during their respective attacks, they are quoted as having the most powerful A.T. Field ever),

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Jan 22, 2008 7:37 pm No, the point is that no other Angel is quoted as having the strongest A.T. Field. It was odd that they should call Ramiel's the "Strongest A.T. Field ever" with only two smaller, weaker Angels to compare. With Kaworu, it was obvious: with Adam's soul aboard, he could manipulate and warp visible light. Normally, they mean something when they make statements like that. Like when Hyuga said, "Eighteen layers of special armor in one hit?" that emphasized Zeruel's incredible destructive capacity. Strangely enough, orbital kamikaze Sahaquiel isn't credited with the "strongest A.T. Field" ever despite having the strongest destructive capabilities of any Angel. As for thread splitting...I'll see if I can split the relevant posts without too much ado.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue Jan 22, 2008 9:02 pm UrsusArctos wrote: No, the point is that no other Angel is quoted as having the strongest A.T. Field. It was odd that they should call Ramiel's the "Strongest A.T. Field ever" with only two smaller, weaker Angels to compare.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Jan 22, 2008 9:21 pm But that still isn't grounds for too valid a comparision: what do they know the rest of the rogue's gallery of Angels?

[]:             Quote Post by V » Tue Jan 22, 2008 9:33 pm No, the point is that no other Angel is quoted as having the strongest A.T. Field. It was odd that they should call Ramiel's the "Strongest A.T. Field ever" with only two smaller, weaker Angels to compare.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Jan 22, 2008 9:39 pm Okay, chuck that...but what about the other stuff? Any of it worth it?

[]:             Quote Post by V » Tue Jan 22, 2008 10:18 pm the comparisons based on alternate spellings of Ramiel's name and other angelic stuff, which are very obscure and are apparently not supported by anything in the series, verge on uberspeculation like the "four demons of hell" thing, etc. It just seems too obscure and supported by no evidence.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue Jan 22, 2008 10:23 pm Where did they say Ramiel had the strongest ATF ever anyways? I don't see anything about that in the platinum subs.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Jan 22, 2008 10:36 pm "Four barons of hell". That was made-up junk. IIRC, that's onscreen text, not in subs anywhere.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue Jan 22, 2008 10:45 pm That makes a big difference. The MAGI must give a literal analysis of the data and Ramiel would have the strongest ATF to date. It's not character opinion it's literal data.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Jan 23, 2008 7:05 am When is this important literal data presented on-screen? Twice- first with Ramiel and then with Kaworu and Rei. Anyway, this thread is as sicko as any of the Imperialists...I'm not a fanw***er and I can't make theories stand up on a thread. (And since the atrocious "Adam and Eve" theory has been deleted, there's no need for this monstrosity)

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Wed Jan 23, 2008 3:09 pm Dramatic effect is your friend, he just needs you to acknowledge him.

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Wed Jan 23, 2008 3:22 pm Kenshiro fought like a hundred different guys that were the strongest ever. It's as if they kept sending in the weakest ones first!

[]:             Quote Post by Themaninblack » Thu Jan 24, 2008 10:23 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: UrsusArctos wrote: No, the point is that no other Angel is quoted as having the strongest A.T. Field. It was odd that they should call Ramiel's the "Strongest A.T. Field ever" with only two smaller, weaker Angels to compare.

[]:             Quote Post by shito » Mon Feb 04, 2008 4:34 pm Ramiel has nothing to do with Kaworu, he's clearly the incarnation of the Blue Water
